creator: brian@freebsd-services.com
text:
> >Number:         32205
> >Category:       bin
> >Synopsis:       PPP login fails in LCP negotiation on option <0x0>

It looks like your ISP has changed their equipment....

The idea behind some ppp implementations sending this <0x0> option is 
that they want to force a rejection so that they have the option of 
sending a further REQ.  I'm not sure of the reasoning behind this.... 
I suspect the peer is expecting us to do something - probably it's 
expecting us to make it clear in our REQ about what our MP 
configuration is.

This stuff hasn't changed in ppp, so I'm fairly sure that your ISP is 
to blame for the breakage.

You've really got two options.

  1.  Tell your ISP to unbreak their software.  This (of course) 
      won't work :)  It may be worth moaning though.

  2.  Muck about some more with your options.  I'd tend to try 
      leaving MRRU disabled and perhaps disable and deny enddisc 
      (some ppp implementations seem to think that enddisc implies 
      MP).

      Another option is to try ``set accmap 000a0000''.  It looks 
      like the peer is using software flow control somewhere along 
      the line.  Perhaps it's expecting you to produce a similar map 
      (the two accmaps are OR'd together after negotiation, so it's 
      not necessary to expect the peer to include your accmap).
-- 
Brian <brian@freebsd-services.com>                <brian@Awfulhak.org>
